content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.5-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the branch diff against 4c3990408bd0caad41b19ef7a86694ccd22e6da6 and ignored .ralph-burning runtime state. Found two correctness issues. nix build passed; a direct cargo test invocation could not start because rustup has no default toolchain configured.

## Amendments

1. src/contexts/bead_workflow/drain.rs:215 and src/contexts/bead_workflow/drain.rs:266-270 make the pending resume path run after a fresh master reset, not on the bead's feature branch. ProcessDrainPorts checks out master in src/cli/drain.rs:153-162, while create_project_from_bead creates a feature branch but the returned branch name is discarded at src/cli/drain.rs:185-204. After a RunInterrupted queues pending_resume_bead, the next loop resets away the branch context and then calls resume_run on master, so a resumed run can lose the work tree and later fail pr open as a non-feature branch. If this is fixed by staying on the feature branch, src/contexts/bead_workflow/drain.rs:231 will still skip the bead because create_project_from_bead marks it in_progress at src/contexts/bead_workflow/create_project.rs:214. Store and restore the created branch for pending resumes, and allow the currently claimed bead status instead of only open.
   Classification: fix_current_bead
   Rationale: The required ResumeOnNextCycle behavior cannot work reliably with the real git/br ports; it either resumes from the wrong branch or skips the claimed bead.
2. src/contexts/bead_workflow/drain.rs:589-591 converts RecoveryAction::Rerun into an Abort/Failed outcome. The bead contract says Rerun is the CI-flake class owned by pr watch and should be ignored by the drain layer; this implementation stops the whole drain if that action reaches handle_recovery_action. Treat it as a drain no-op/continue case, or make it an explicit unreachable invariant before classification can return it here, instead of surfacing Failed.
   Classification: fix_current_bead
   Rationale: This is a direct mismatch with the specified RecoveryAction handling and can turn a known flake classification into a terminal drain failure.
